denial_pattern_sources: algo_time_delta: ProPublica + Capitol Forum Cigna Investigation 2023; Senate Finance Committee UHC Investigation 2024
unreasonable_documentation_deadline: DOL ERISA Claims Procedure Regulations 29 CFR § 2560.503-1(g); AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Survey
denial_letter_text:
Dear Plan Member,

We received your provider's request on 04/08/2026 for Septal myectomy evaluation related to Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (I42.2). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Annual visit limit for services treating Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (I42.2) has been exceeded. Additional visits are not a covered benefit this plan year.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with visit limit exceeded. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.
clinical_context: The 34-year-old member has Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (I42.2). She has been treated under commercial coverage with Septal myectomy evaluation recommended as the next appropriate step. LVOT gradient 65 mmHg on stress echo with NYHA class III symptoms. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that visit limit exceeded bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic UHC correspondence with UnitedHealthcare medical policy and procedural tone.
